relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks severe holiness boundaries.
Giving children to Molech is treated as a life-destroying profanation.
YHWH sets his face against child-sacrifice because it defiles sanctuary and Name.
Turning away from such harm is itself condemned.
Molech devotion corrupts clan and people.
Seeking forbidden spirit powers is treated as covenant betrayal.
Sanctify yourselves and be holy.
YHWH sanctifies through statutes done.
Cursing parents is treated as grave covenant rupture.
Adultery is judged as serious betrayal.
The father’s household boundary is guarded severely.
The daughter-in-law boundary is guarded severely.
The male-male penalty text is preserved for deep audit, never for harm.
A woman and her mother are not to be taken together.
Human-animal violation is rejected.
The human-animal boundary is guarded.
Sibling nakedness is named disgrace and public rupture.
Menstrual boundary violation brings cutting-off language.
Aunt boundaries are guarded.
The uncle’s marriage boundary is guarded.
The brother’s marriage boundary is guarded.
The statutes are tied to dwelling in the land.
Israel is warned not to walk in prior nations’ patterns.
YHWH separates the people for a land of milk and honey.
Animal distinctions continue the holiness pattern.
Be holy to YHWH, who separates you as his own.
Spirit-practice penalty material is preserved with caution against modern persecution.

contemplative_note: Leviticus 20 is one of the hardest holiness chapters. Its safest mystical use is not punishment, hate, or fear, but sober refusal of child-sacrifice, exploitation, betrayal, coercion, and all weaponized readings that harm living people.
